prospect.title: Head of Marketing
prospect.personalization_hook: At ScaleOps, content consistency is probably a challenge at 28 headcount.

sequence.subject: Your content operation, Morgan
sequence.body:
Hi Morgan,

Most Head of Marketings I talk to are spending $2,000-4,000/month on content. Writers, VAs, social scheduling — it adds up fast and the output is still inconsistent.

I replaced all of it with 63 AI bots for $70/month. The system publishes daily without me.

Worth a 5-minute look? nyspotlightreport.com/proflow/

— SC Thomas

sequence.message: Hi Morgan — SC Thomas from NY Spotlight Report. I help Head of Marketings cut content costs 90% with AI automation. Would love to connect.

sequence.subject: From $4k/month to $70/month (case study)
sequence.body:
Hi Morgan,

Quick case study since I mentioned it:

6 months ago: $4,200/month (writer, VA, social manager, tools)
Today: $187/month — 63 AI bots, publishes daily, zero management

Output actually improved. Consistency went from 3x/week to 7x/week.

Is ScaleOps dealing with the same cost/consistency problem?

— SC

sequence.subject: 15 minutes?
sequence.body:
Hi Morgan,

I'll be direct: I think we can cut your content costs by 80% and make output more consistent.

If I'm wrong, the call costs you 15 minutes. If I'm right, it's worth a lot more.

Cal link: calendly.com/nyspotlightreport

— SC

sequence.subject: Closing the loop
sequence.body:
Hi Morgan,

I'll stop after this — don't want to be that person.

If you're happy with your current content operation, completely understood.

If the cost/consistency problem is still there in Q2, nyspotlightreport.com/proflow/ will be here.

All the best,
— SC Thomas

objection_handlers.too_expensive: You're paying $X/month in time. $97 replaces that and gives you better output.
objection_handlers.not_interested: Totally fine. Quick question before I go — what's your current content cost/month?
objection_handlers.send_me_info: Sending the overview now. What's the one thing you'd need to see to know if it's worth a call?
objection_handlers.already_have_someone: Smart. What would make you consider switching — is it cost, output quality, or something else?
objection_handlers.bad_timing: Understood. When would be better — Q2, after the [event], or when you're next evaluating tools?
